checklist.claim: registry path == file location == run.sh operand
floor line shape matches runner regex
four lib/test files shellcheck-clean in CI scope
over-broad module write cannot erase earlier verdict
every later EXIT trap preserves tally files
registry test_modules key shape read with exact spellings
registry path points at existing module file

phase3_findings.description: test_selector_diagnostic_temp_is_removed_before_module_execution assumes bare mktemp honors TMPDIR override — fails on macOS/BSD (suite RED locally, green on Linux CI)
phase3_findings.note: also raised by devflow:requesting-code-review as Critical (kind logic_error — signature kind mismatch, prose-identical); severity-calibrated to Important; confirmed by orchestrator in-env suite run 1

phase3_findings.description: test_focused_python_failure_prints_captured_diagnostics asserts plain traceback substring — fails under FORCE_COLOR / Python 3.13+ colorized tracebacks
phase3_findings.note: also raised by devflow:requesting-code-review (kind logic_error); orchestrator interpreter-faithful probe confirmed FORCE_COLOR=3 + Python 3.14 root cause

phase3_findings.description: Engine-surface PR carries no .changeset/*.md (issue #290 policy — Phase 3 gate FAILs engine-surface changes without one)

Module contract comment claims caller needs only LIB/RESULTS_FILE/assert_eq and module 'owns all other fixtures' — false against HEAD: module calls devflow_run_focused_python_test defined only in module-harness.sh (self-contradicting diff, non-demotable)
awk-extracted Stop-hook doc excerpt has no positive control — empty extraction passes the no-recorder-command assertion vacuously (extension guard-class 2/3)
Single-run issue-block rejection asserted only by exit code; captured single-issue.err never read (extension guard-class 3)
Workspace dir leaked when mktemp succeeds but validation rejects it (trap installed after validation)
run-module.sh cleanup only on EXIT trap; untrapped fatal signal leaks temp files
Module-boundary FAIL diagnostics go to stderr while sibling suite FAILs go to stdout (split-stream diagnosability)
MIN_ASSERTIONS tuple field not re-validated in focused runner — malformed multi-line value makes [ -lt ] error inside elif and skips the floor gate (fail-open)
Trap-composition pin asserts only IMPL_SKILL_BUNDLE — a trap dropping MODULE_FAILURES_FILE/SKIPS_FILE stays green
phase3_findings.note: raised by both pr-test-analyzer and type-design-analyzer (line ranges differ — one used diff positions; prose-identical)

phase3_findings.description: Focused-runner green happy path (real runner + real registry + real module) never executed by any test — environment-contract regressions ship green
test_parent_failure_flag_does_not_contaminate_unforced_child_runs is vacuous — the helper scrubs the env var the test claims to exercise
Writing-skills evidence gate (consumer extension): diff touches .devflow/prompt-extensions/*.md; no linked issue and empty PR body carry no 'Writing-skills evidence:' marker — FAIL closed
phase3_findings.note: orchestrator-verified (extension gate); discharge surface is the PR description

phase3_findings.description: ci.yml comment categorizes the module body as orchestration/rendering code — imprecise (true-but-awkward)
Overview omits the --log-dir override when describing the persistent log location
Unbounded persistent log accumulation under .devflow/tmp/test-module-logs/ (no pruning)
Harness subshell shields MODULE_FAILURES_FILE but not SKIPS_FILE — module could append skips outside the sanctioned helper
phase3_findings.note: also raised by devflow:requesting-code-review (kind test_gap — kind mismatch, prose-identical)

phase3_findings.description: sanitize_result_field empty placeholder renders literal backslashes: \(empty\)
Relative --registry/--log-dir normalization arms untested
devflow_fold_module_failures non-numeric operand arm untested

Completeness critic (detect_all_audit): registry/full-suite coupling audit is complete w.r.t. independent enumeration (registry keys; modules/ directory listing) but pins the single member by hardcoded name — a future second registry entry falls outside it

fix_decisions.claim_text: macOS mktemp/TMPDIR test failure graded Critical by final-pass
fix_decisions.evidence: over-grade shape 1: the defect's own failure mode is a loud suite-RED (bounded stop) that blocks the local gate — real and fixed this iteration, but not silent corruption; recorded evaluation, not an auto-demote

fix_decisions.claim_text: boundary FAIL diagnostics on stderr vs suite FAILs on stdout
fix_decisions.evidence: real but minor diagnosability polish; changing streams would break the harness Python tests' stderr assertions and is a convention decision, not a correctness fix — count is never wrong (fold is fail-closed)

fix_decisions.note: discharged via PR-description marker (see Devflow Reflection) — evidence is the eight #563 assert_pin_red_under mutation pins over the extension sections' operative sentences

fix_decisions.evidence: real but minor: logs live in gitignored .devflow/tmp scratch; a pruning policy is a design decision (keep-N vs age) not worth a fix-loop iteration

fix_decisions.claim_text: coupling audit pins the single registry member by hardcoded name
fix_decisions.evidence: no uncovered member exists today (1 file <-> 1 entry, superset holds); generalizing the audit to iterate registry members is future-proofing, not a current defect per Phase 3.1.5 step 4

test_first_gate.evidence: F-1/F-2 red tests observed failing pre-fix, green post-fix (suite run 2: 8271 passed, 0 failed)
